danger_signals.visual_form: Goenitz depicted as supernatural antagonist with tornado/wind effects; visual distinction from tournament fighters
danger_signals.meaning: Presence of supernatural threat to world order; Orochi demon servant represents existential danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Health gauge flashing red when low
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger to player character; vulnerability state enabling emergency power mechanics

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss Team featuring Geese Howard, Wolfgang Krauser, Mr. Big with distinct visual styling from other fighters
danger_signals.meaning: Concentrated threat faction; antagonistic forces united against protagonist teams

safety_signals.visual_form: Formal King of Fighters tournament bracket and team organization display
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled, regulated combat environment; legitimacy and order maintained by Chizuru Kagura

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-selected three-fighter teams with shared visual/thematic coherence
safety_signals.meaning: Strength in numbers and alliance; player agency in assembling protective force

safety_signals.visual_form: Tournament host character presence; hereditary authority marker
safety_signals.meaning: Establishment legitimacy and protective institutional framework against chaos

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kusanagi lineage heritage; Kyo Kusanagi as protagonist representative
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: One of three clans responsible for sealing Orochi demon; ancient spiritual authority

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Iori Yagami as clan representative; bitter rival dynamic with Kusanagi
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Yagami Team includes Mature and Vice (former Rugal assistants), indicating darker affiliations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Second of three sealing clans; tainted by curse or dark lineage implications

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chizuru Kagura as heir; tournament authority figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Third of three sealing clans; hosts and organizes tournament; institutional power

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Goenitz as primary antagonist; supernatural aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goenitz depicted with tornado/wind visual effects distinguishing from normal fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Represent existential threat from sealed demon; actively working against sealing efforts

faction_indicators.faction: Fatal Fury/Art of Fighting Villains (Boss Team)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Geese Howard, Wolfgang Krauser, Mr. Big; established antagonists from crossover IPs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct visual styling marking them as imported villain faction with external origins
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: United as special antagonist team; concentrated dangerous force against player progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Leona joins as new member (replacing Heidern mentor figure)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military-affiliated team; represents organized power structure beyond clans

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Yuri Sakazaki as new representative; replaced previous lineup
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Represents Art of Fighting universe integration; generational shift in representation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kasumi Todoh as new member (Yuri's replacement position)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rotating roster; represents gender-diverse fighting force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goenitz boss character appearance with supernatural visual effects; tournament announced as counter-measure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goenitz actively opposes tournament and ambushes Chizuru; demon servants work to prevent sealing ritual

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chizuru Kagura replaces Rugal Bernstein as tournament host; institutional shift in authority
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New invitation system; reformed fighter roster; tournament redirected toward demon sealing rather than pure competition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three clans (Kusanagi, Yagami, Yata) brought into narrative prominence through character presence and plot focus
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kyo and Iori (rival clan members) forced into alliance if player advances with Chizuru; supernatural cooperation over traditional enmity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss Team featuring Fatal Fury and Art of Fighting villains with distinct visual import markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: External antagonistic faction operates within tournament; represents meta-narrative threat convergence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health bar flashing red; power gauge visual prominence increases; screen UI emphasizes critical state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains access to Maximum power special moves when both conditions met (low health + full gauge); gameplay shifts to emergency mode

environmental_storytelling.example: Removal of Heidern and Takuma from tournament rosters signals retirement/generational shift; Leona and Yuri replace them, indicating passing of authority to younger generation
Mature and Vice joining Iori Yagami team (after serving Rugal) signals corrupted allegiance and darker forces accumulating around Yagami clan
Geese Howard, Krauser, Mr. Big grouped as unified antagonist team rather than individual opponents communicates concentrated external threat
Kyo and Iori prominence in narrative and tournament structure visualizes ancient clan conflict being weaponized for modern demon-sealing purpose
Formal invitation system and organized bracket structure (vs. previous Rugal's dominion) establishes institutional protection against chaos

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Ambushes Chizuru, actively opposes tournament progression, represents direct Orochi threat manifestation
Sought by Chizuru for recruitment; targeted by Goenitz; must be allied with Iori for optimal ending sequence
Bitter rival of Kyo but forced into cooperation through Chizuru's sealing plans; flanked by corrupted Mature and Vice
Recruits fighters for demon-sealing mission; tests winners; can be unlocked as playable character indicating dual role
Health bar flashes red; gains access to enhanced Super Special Moves when power gauge maxed; visual indicator of desperation mechanics
Operate as concentrated opposition team; visually distinguished from tournament fighters; represent meta-threat convergence

notes: KOF '96 primarily communicates world-state through narrative/roster composition rather than environmental visuals (fighting game constraint). Danger signals manifest through boss character presence and health mechanics rather than environment hazards. Faction identity relies on character lineage, team composition, and plot positioning. The game uses a three-clan mythological framework (Kusanagi, Yagami, Yata) as core world-state visual/narrative communication. Boss Team acts as visual 'anomaly' marker. UI elements (health flashing, power gauge prominence) serve dual purpose as both mechanical signals and danger indicators.
